findings.message: DeleteButton performs an irreversible destructive action with no confirmation step. A misclick immediately fires the DELETE request with no way to cancel or undo.
findings.suggestion: Add a window.confirm() prompt or a custom confirmation dialog (e.g. 'Are you sure you want to delete this item?') before calling fetch, so users can cancel accidental clicks.

findings.message: No disabled-while-pending state: the button remains clickable while the DELETE request is in flight, allowing duplicate requests on rapid clicks.
findings.suggestion: Track an `isPending` state variable; set it to true before the fetch and false after, and pass `disabled={isPending}` to the button to prevent double-submission.

findings.message: No error feedback on failed delete: if the fetch call throws or returns a non-2xx status, window.location.reload() is still called and no error message is shown to the user.
findings.suggestion: Check the response status (response.ok) inside handleDelete and, on failure, set an error state and render an error message instead of silently reloading the page.
